prompt_quality.reasoning: SYSTEM_PROMPT (agent.py:11-54) is well-structured (role + framework + constraints) but contains heavy redundancy. Competitor rule is stated THREE times: system prompt (agent.py:46-52), packet section header (agent.py:195 'the ONLY product names you may mention'), and enrich_persona prompt (gemini.py:252-257). 'EVALUATE not EXPAND' is restated 3x: SYSTEM_PROMPT (agent.py:23-37), execution instructions 7-10 (agent.py:227-230), reflection loop (agent.py:244-246). Schema (review-output-schema.json) orders 'score' before 'reason' inside each scores object (lines 24-31), violating reasoning-before-commit best practice. Three diverging request builders (gemini.py:139-212) produce non-deterministic prompt shapes across retries (systemInstruction vs inline vs plain-text), making failure mode attribution hard. Embedded schema block at end of packet (agent.py:250-253) is rendered as markdown JSON rather than a real responseSchema. No examples provided. Prompt-cache-friendly: prefix IS relatively stable (system prompt + journey stages) but the Retry Note is appended to the same user turn rather than kept as a separate message, weakening cache hits on retry.

llm_configuration.reasoning: Critical anti-patterns. No 'temperature' is set anywhere (grep across src found zero hits) — Gemini 2.5 Pro default is ~1.0 which is far too high for strict JSON output; best practice is 0.0-0.3. `responseSchema` is NEVER set in any of the 3 run_review builders (gemini.py:139-212) nor in enrich_persona (gemini.py:307-317), despite `build_response_json_schema` being computed (agent.py:102, 260) and persisted as `packet.response_json_schema` — it is written to disk but never attached to the API call. The schema is instead embedded inline as a markdown JSON block (agent.py:250-253), which Google docs explicitly warn produces lower-quality output. `responseMimeType: application/json` is set (gemini.py:166, 190, 310, 316) — good but insufficient without responseSchema. Model `gemini-2.5-pro` is hardcoded for both stages (gemini.py:17; service.py:17,84,138); enrich_persona (13-field persona card) is over-provisioned — gemini-2.5-flash would suffice at ~5-10x lower cost. Google Search tool is enabled in only the first run_review builder and the first enrich_persona attempt (gemini.py:164, 309), which is reasonable. No top-p/top-k tuning. Timeout 45s (gemini.py:20) is reasonable for 2.5-pro. The 3rd run_review builder (plain_text_fallback, gemini.py:195-212) drops `responseMimeType` entirely — this is a quality regression path, not a useful fallback.

retry_reliability.reasoning: run_review retry loop (gemini.py:39-73) runs 3 builders × 2 attempts = up to 6 calls with NO time.sleep, NO exponential backoff, NO jitter — a synchronous retry storm against a rate-limited endpoint. NO error-class discrimination: _make_request (gemini.py:81-102) raises a blanket GeminiError for HTTPError and URLError alike, so HTTP 400 (bad request, permanent), 401/403 (auth, permanent), 429 (rate limit, transient with Retry-After), 500/503 (transient), content-safety blocks (permanent), and network timeouts all retry identically. `Retry-After` header is never parsed (urllib response.headers is discarded on error). On a 400 or quota-exhausted key, all 6 attempts burn quota and latency before surfacing. PARTIAL CREDIT: validation-failure path does inject previous_failure into next prompt (gemini.py:50, 59, 65, 72) — the one correct piece. Counter-bug: on API error, `last_response_json` is not cleared, so error text can be stale. enrich_persona retry (gemini.py:319-326) is only 2 attempts with a blanket `except (GeminiError, json.JSONDecodeError)` that silently eats BOTH bad-JSON and HTTP errors with no distinction. No circuit breaker, no fallback model. Content-safety blocks will loop for 6 attempts and still fail.

pipeline_structure.reasoning: Duplicate upstream work: `fetch_webpage_context` is called in `generate_persona_from_form` (service.py:88) AND again in `build_packet_for_brief` (service.py:18) for the same URL within a single end-to-end request — confirmed 2x HTTP crawl of up to 8 pages each = up to 16 page fetches. No caching layer between them. No persona cache keyed by (service_name, service_type, persona_description) hash despite persona enrichment being deterministic on inputs. Schema is computed via `build_response_json_schema` (agent.py:102-118) → stored on `ReviewPacket.response_json_schema` (agent.py:96) → written to skeleton_output (service.py:40-43) but NEVER passed to the Gemini API as `responseSchema` parameter; the prompt-embedded schema (agent.py:250-253) is used instead. Sequential topology (crawl → enrich_persona → crawl again → run_review) is reasonable for a single request but has the avoidable duplicate crawl. No post-review critic pass exists, which is fine for cost. Raw request dump (gemini.py:42-43) is overwritten on each builder attempt, losing earlier-attempt traces for debugging.

token_efficiency.reasoning: Quantified waste: (1) Schema block embedded in prompt (agent.py:250-253) — the review-output-schema.json is ~80 lines ≈ 500-800 tokens per call, fully removable by using provider responseSchema. (2) Webpage context: 8 pages × 2200 chars = ~17.6k chars ≈ 4.4k tokens per call with NO relevance filtering against the persona/journey (webpage.py:14-15, 133). (3) Competitor rule restated 3x across SYSTEM_PROMPT / packet header / enrich_persona ≈ 300-500 redundant tokens. (4) 'EVALUATE not EXPAND' restated 3x (system prompt, exec instructions 7-10, reflection loop) ≈ 200-300 redundant tokens. (5) SYSTEM_PROMPT is embedded into the packet markdown (agent.py:169-170) AND in builder #1 it is ALSO sent via `systemInstruction` (gemini.py:141-153) — partial duplication of the role framing when that builder wins. (6) VALIDATION_CRITERIA (agent.py:57-65) is spelled out in reflection loop plus additional custom criteria — verbose. (7) Request dump file grows unboundedly on retries. Rough baseline per request: ~6-8k input tokens, of which ~1.5-2k is removable without quality loss. No relevance filter on crawled text. No semantic dedup across crawled pages.

target_rationale: Budget is 5 iterations (current + 4 remaining). Three critical criteria (prompt_quality, llm_configuration, retry_reliability) each have well-scoped, concrete fixes: (1) wire up `responseSchema` + add `temperature: 0.1` (single-file edit in gemini.py + agent.py, unlocks +1.5-2 points on llm_configuration and removes schema-in-prompt waste), (2) dedupe the competitor rule and EVALUATE-not-EXPAND restatements, reorder scores schema to reason-before-score (+1 point on prompt_quality, +0.5 on token_efficiency), (3) add error-class discrimination + exponential backoff with jitter + Retry-After parsing + stop-on-permanent-error (+1.5 points on retry_reliability). Pipeline_structure improvement (persona caching, deduplicate fetch) is a single mechanical fix worth +1 point. Token_efficiency benefits automatically from (1) and (2). 4.0 is realistic; 4.5 would require provider-native tooling refactor beyond budget. 3.5 would be the floor if retry work is harder than expected due to urllib's limited header access (may need requests/httpx migration).
feedback: The pipeline works but leaves substantial Gemini-engineering value on the table. Biggest lift: move the embedded JSON schema out of the prompt and into `generationConfig.responseSchema` — the code already BUILDS the schema object (`build_response_json_schema`) and writes it to disk, but never attaches it to the API call. Adding this plus `temperature: 0.1` should meaningfully reduce JSON parse/validation failures and shrink per-call tokens by ~500-800. Second biggest lift: retry logic is an unconditional 6-attempt loop with no sleep and no HTTP status classification — a 400 or content-safety block burns 6 calls. Introduce error classes (retryable_transient vs permanent vs validation) and exponential backoff with jitter; parse Retry-After on 429. Third: the competitor rule and the EVALUATE-not-EXPAND framing are each stated three times in different places — keep one canonical statement in SYSTEM_PROMPT and remove the restatements in execution instructions, reflection loop, and enrich_persona. Fourth (important-tier): fetch_webpage_context is called twice per request (persona + review) — memoize by URL for the duration of a request, and consider persona caching by input hash. Fifth (detail-tier): `review-output-schema.json` orders `score` before `reason` inside each scoring block; flip this so the model writes reasoning before committing to a numeric score. Minor: the third fallback builder drops responseMimeType entirely, which is a regression, not a fallback — remove it or keep the mime type.

priority_fixes.suggestion: In all 3 run_review builders (gemini.py:139-212) and in enrich_persona (gemini.py:307-317), add `responseSchema` to `generationConfig` using the already-computed `packet.response_json_schema` (plumb it through run_review signature and _make_request). Add `temperature: 0.1` and `topP: 0.95` to every generationConfig. Remove the embedded schema JSON block from the packet markdown (agent.py:250-253) since responseSchema + description fields replace it. Switch enrich_persona to `gemini-2.5-flash` by default (retain pro as override) to save ~5-10x cost on persona stage. Remove or fix the 3rd fallback builder `build_request_plain_text_fallback` (gemini.py:195-212) — it drops responseMimeType, which is a regression not a fallback; either add responseMimeType back or delete this builder entirely.

In `_make_request` (gemini.py:81-102), capture HTTP status code and Retry-After header into a structured GeminiError subclass (e.g., GeminiTransientError vs GeminiPermanentError vs GeminiRateLimitError with retry_after_seconds). In the run_review retry loop (gemini.py:39-73): (a) break immediately on permanent errors (400 bad request, 401/403 auth, content-safety block in response finishReason); (b) on 429, sleep for Retry-After header or default; (c) on 5xx/timeout, sleep with exponential backoff base=1s factor=2x + full jitter (random 0-backoff), capped at ~30s; (d) cap total attempts at 3, not 6 — the 3 builder × 2 attempt structure is excessive; (e) keep the existing validation-failure path that injects previous_failure (this part is correct). For enrich_persona (gemini.py:319-326), apply the same classification and drop the blanket `except (GeminiError, json.JSONDecodeError)` in favor of targeted handlers. Consider moving from urllib to `requests` or `httpx` to make header access ergonomic.

Dedupe the competitor rule to ONE canonical statement in SYSTEM_PROMPT (agent.py:46-52); delete the restatement in the 'Known Competitors' section header (agent.py:195) leaving only the data, and delete the 'CRITICAL RULE ON COMPETITORS' block in enrich_persona prompt (gemini.py:252-257). Dedupe the 'EVALUATE not EXPAND' framing to its single canonical home in SYSTEM_PROMPT (agent.py:23-37); remove items 7-10 in execution instructions (agent.py:227-230) and the three bullets in reflection loop (agent.py:244-246) that restate the same point. Reorder `review-output-schema.json` scores fields so `reason` precedes `score` (reasoning-before-commit) — change each scores entry from `{score, reason}` to `{reason, score}`. Once responseSchema is in place (per llm_configuration fix), remove the schema markdown block from the packet (agent.py:250-253). Consolidate the 3 divergent request builders down to ONE canonical shape (systemInstruction + contents + full generationConfig) to make failure modes deterministic across retries.
